metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how `code_review_decision` bypasses or overrides the per-repo `verify.enable_code_review` gate when the Gerrit voter is active — e.g. the adapter constructs a `LLMConfig`/config override with `enable_code_review=True` (since voter activation is already controlled by `[gerrit_voter].enabled` + allowlist, the per-repo flag is a redundant second gate in this context) — and add a test asserting the voter fires even when the reviewed repo has no `rebar.toml`.
findings.finding: The `code_review_decision` adapter wraps `produce_code_review_verdict`, which internally calls `code_review_enabled(repo_root)` — reading `verify.enable_code_review` from the *reviewed repo's* `rebar.toml`. That flag defaults to `false`. When the Gerrit voter activates via `[gerrit_voter].enabled=true` + allowlist, the adapter calls `produce_code_review_verdict` against a repo that almost certainly has `verify.enable_code_review=false`, so the function returns the inert PASS verdict (`coverage.enabled=false`). The adapter then maps this to a `-1` (gate-disabled coverage-gap) on every patchset, making the voter permanently fail-closed regardless of `[gerrit_voter]` configuration. The plan does not describe how the adapter overrides or bypasses the per-repo `verify.enable_code_review` gate.
findings.suggested_fix: In `code_review_decision`, construct the config/runner with `enable_code_review=True` explicitly (the voter's own `[gerrit_voter]` config is the activation gate; the per-repo flag is a user-facing opt-in for the CLI/MCP path, not the Gerrit voter path). Alternatively, accept an `LLMConfig` parameter with `enable_code_review` forced true by the patchset handler before calling the adapter.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the patchset-created handler obtains `repo_root`: e.g., a configured static path, an on-demand git-clone step, or an event-payload field — so the handler's interface is fully determined without executor invention.
findings.finding: The patchset-created handler requires a `repo_root` argument (for `code_review_decision` → `produce_code_review_verdict`) but the plan never specifies how the handler acquires the local checkout path. The REST API surface section describes diff-fetch via `GET .../patch` but is silent on where `repo_root` comes from in the handler flow — leaving an executor to invent this design detail.
findings.location: Gerrit REST API surface section / AC3 (patchset-created handler)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'Gerrit REST API surface' or 'What' section: e.g. 'The receiver is configured with a `repo_root` base path (config key `gerrit_voter.repo_root`); for stub tests a tmp directory is injected. On-demand git-clone is out of scope for WS6-a.' Alternatively, if repo_root is unused in the REST-only patchset handler (and WS4 operates purely on diff_text when repo_root is empty/None), state that explicitly so the executor knows to pass an empty string or None.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which Gerrit event-delivery mechanism triggers the patchset-created handler (hooks plugin / webhooks plugin / SSH stream-events / REST polling), justify the choice against the alternatives, and confirm it is compatible with the d251-provisioned Gerrit instance.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a 'patchset-created handler' in `src/rebar/gerrit/` but never specifies which Gerrit event-delivery mechanism triggers it. Gerrit offers at least four competing approaches — the native `hooks` plugin (local script execution), SSH stream-events (daemon), the `webhooks` plugin (HTTP POST to a receiver URL), and REST polling — each with different deployment topology, reliability guarantees, and at-least-once semantics. The plan is silent on which mechanism is used and why, even though this is the architectural crux of the voter's integration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and test the diff-fetch failure path: define what the patchset handler does when GET /patch or /files fails (e.g., treat as coverage-gap INDETERMINATE → -1 with `coverage-gap (diff-unavailable)` tag, same as LLM outage), and add a stub-driven AC covering a diff-fetch failure scenario analogous to AC6's vote-POST failure test.
findings.finding: The diff-fetch failure path (GET /patch or /files returning 4xx/5xx/timeout) is unspecified and untested: the plan states retry/fail-fast behavior for the REST client generically but AC6 scopes its stub-driven failure test only to 'vote POST'. No AC or prose specifies what the patchset handler returns/logs when diff-fetch fails, leaving a gap in both the behavioral contract and the test coverage for this new user-facing flow.
findings.location: AC3 ('stub records the posted {labels, message} and serves the canned diff') + AC6 ('vote POST that ultimately fails') + 'REST vote failure behavior' section
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the 'REST vote failure behavior' section to also cover diff-fetch failure: 'If GET .../patch or .../files fails after retries, the handler treats the patchset as INDETERMINATE → decision=BLOCK → -1 with coverage-gap tag `diff-unavailable`, logs the error, and returns a DiffFetchError result (no partial state).' Add a corresponding AC line or fold it into AC6: 'a stub-driven test also asserts that a diff-fetch failure yields a coverage-gap -1 (diff-unavailable) and no vote is silently dropped.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named fallback for diff-fetch failure (GET /patch or /files 4xx/5xx/timeout) to the handler behavior section and a corresponding stub-driven test in AC3 or AC6 — specify what the handler returns (e.g., a `DiffFetchError` result, or a coverage-gap `-1` vote) when the diff-fetch sub-step fails before the adapter is called.
findings.finding: The diff-fetch sub-step (GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch and GET .../files) has no specified handler-level fallback for failure: the 'REST vote failure behavior' section and AC6 only cover vote POST failure exhaustively, but the plan never states what the patchset handler returns or does when the diff-fetch itself fails (e.g., 404 change not found, 503 transient, auth 401). A diff-fetch failure is a distinct, earlier sub-step failure that prevents the adapter from even being called — the plan leaves this path unspecified and untested.
findings.location: REST vote failure behavior section; AC3; AC6
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the 'REST vote failure behavior' section with: 'Diff-fetch failure (GET /patch or /files ultimately fails after retries) ⇒ the handler logs the error and returns a `DiffFetchError` result; no `LLM-Review` vote is recorded (fail-closed by the same missing-vote construction as VoteError).' Add a stub-driven test in AC3 where the stub returns a 503 on the diff endpoint and assert the handler returns DiffFetchError with no vote posted.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `label_name: str = "LLM-Review"` to the `[gerrit_voter]` config section (alongside `max_vote`) and reference it in the REST vote POST body, so that a label rename is a config-only change.
findings.finding: The Gerrit label name 'LLM-Review' used in the REST vote POST body is hardcoded in the implementation rather than being a config value, even though its identity is provisioned externally by d251. If the label name changes (d251 re-provisions under a different name, or a second label is added), a code change is required instead of a config update.
findings.location: Gerrit REST API surface section — ReviewInput JSON: `{"labels": {"LLM-Review": <label_value>}}`
findings.suggested_fix: In the [gerrit_voter] config section add: `label_name: str = "LLM-Review"`. Use `config.gerrit_voter.label_name` when constructing the ReviewInput JSON body. This mirrors the existing pattern for `max_vote`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit activation prerequisite to the Staged opt-in section (or ADR 0013): 'd251's label, submit requirement, and service account MUST be provisioned in the target Gerrit instance before enabling_projects is non-empty and enabled is set to true.'
findings.finding: The deploy ordering constraint between this voter code and d251's provisioned Gerrit artifacts (label, submit requirement, service account) is not explicitly documented. The plan relies on `enabled=false` as an implicit guard but does not state that d251 must be fully provisioned BEFORE `enabled` is flipped to true during first activation.
findings.location: Dependencies section and Staged opt-in + rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Staged opt-in + rollback' section, add a bullet: 'Activation prerequisite: d251's LLM-Review label, =MAX submit requirement, and service-account HTTP password MUST be provisioned before the first project is added to enabled_projects. Enabling the voter before provisioning will cause vote POSTs to fail with 4xx and is not a safe state.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify handler-level idempotency for at-least-once patchset-created delivery: either document that Gerrit SetReview's last-write-wins semantics are acceptable (and that duplicate LLM calls are tolerated), or add a deduplication guard (e.g., record processed (change-id, revision-id) pairs, or check existing vote before calling the gate), and add a stub test asserting idempotent behavior on duplicate invocation.
findings.finding: The patchset-created handler does not address idempotency under at-least-once event delivery. Gerrit event streams and hook supervisors commonly redeliver patchset-created events (on restart, network partition, or retry). If the handler fires twice for the same (change-id, revision-id) pair, it will issue two LLM gate calls and two vote POSTs. While Gerrit's SetReview is idempotent for identical label values, two concurrent invocations could race and produce non-deterministic final vote state if LLM responses differ across calls (e.g., transient outage clears between invocations). The plan specifies retry semantics for the REST client but is silent on handler-level deduplication.
findings.location: Patchset-created handler / REST vote failure behavior section / AC3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the patchset handler spec: 'Handler MUST be idempotent: if a vote for (change_id, revision_id) has already been successfully posted, a duplicate invocation MUST be a no-op (skip LLM call and vote POST). Implement by checking the Gerrit change's existing LLM-Review label value before invoking the gate, or by maintaining a local processed-set keyed on (change_id, revision_id) with TTL matching the patchset lifecycle.' Add a stub test case: invoke the handler twice for the same patchset; assert exactly one LLM call and one vote POST are made.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
